title: crotalaria rhodesiae
intro: Crotalaria rhodesiae, also known as Bushman's poison or Poison rattle bush, is a plant species native to southern Africa, belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. The health benefits of Crotalaria rhodesiae include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions, and its seeds are also used to make a medicinal oil, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
